content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

December 26, 1986


Dave Terrill
Fair Management Consultant
Division of Fairs and Expositions
Department of Food and Agriculture
1010 Hurley Way, Suite 200
Sacramento, CA  95825

Re:  Your Request for Informal
Assistance
Our File No. I-86-337

Dear Mr. Terrill:

You have written on behalf of your agency seeking informal
assistance with respect to conflict of interest issues which
might arise under the Political Reform Act (the "Act").1/

QUESTION

Is there any conflict of interest under the Act if your
agency simultaneously contracts with Simulcast Enterprises,
Inc., to transmit off-track-betting signals, and with its
president, Allan Balch, to manage a horse show for one of the
district fairs at which the simulcast will occur?

CONCLUSION

Under the facts presented, two separate, simultaneous
contracts of the nature described would not represent a
conflict of interest under the Act.

_____________________

1/ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise
indicated. Commission regulations appear at 2 California
Administrative Code Sections 18000, et seq. All references to
regulations are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California
Administrative Code.

Our advice is limited to the provisions of the Act. For
advice on other provisions of law, such as Section 1090 and the
state contracting law, your agency should contact the Attorney
General's office.

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916)322-5660

Dave Terrill
December 26, 1986
Page Two


FACTS

The Earl Warren Showgrounds (19th District Agricultural
Association) intends to enter into an agreement with Simulcast
Enterprises, Inc., to handle the transmission of simulcast
wagering signals ("off-track betting") to their showgrounds.
This contract is being negotiated by the California Authority
of Racing Fairs (on behalf of the 19th D.A.A.) along with
agreements for several other fairs.

At the same time, Allan Balch, president of Simulcast
Enterprises, Inc., is being offered a contract (as an
individual) to manage a horse show for the 19th D.A.A. in
July. Mr. Balch is not an officer or employee of the 19th
D.A.A. and has no involvement in the selection process for
either of the contracts in question, nor will he have any
decision-making authority as horse show manager that will
impact in any way on his own income from his company's
simulcasting work with the 19th D.A.A..

ANALYSIS

The Act prohibits any "public official" from making, parti-
cipating in making, or using his official position to influence
any governmental decision in which he has a financial interest
(Section 87100.). A financial interest in a decision exists
when the provisions of Section 87103 (copy enclosed) are met.

In this instance, Mr. Balch is performing a specific and
limited function over a relatively short time period (i.e., the
duration of the fair). In his individual role as horse show
manager, he has no role in making governmental decisions
regarding the agreement with his company, Simulcast. Those
decisions, as well as the decision to hire Mr. Balch to manage
the horse show, are being made by public officials who are
employees of your agency. We have been provided with no facts
to indicate that any of these public officials have any
financial interest in either of these decisions.

Should you have any questions regarding this advice, you
may reach me at (916) 322-5901.

Sincerely,

Diane M. Griffiths
General Counsel

By: Robert E. Leidigh
Counsel, Legal Division

DMG:REL:plh

State of California

M e m o r a n d u m

To     :  Robert Leidigh, Counsel
           Legal Division
           Fair Political Practices Commission
           P. O. Box 807
           Sacramento 95804


From   :  Department of Food and Agriculture – 1010 Hurley Way, Suite 200
                                                  Sacramento 95825

Subject:  Conflict of Interest Opinion


           Thank you for the information you gave me during our recent conversation and
your offer to reiterate, in writing, your opinion.

     To briefly restate the issue, the Earl Warren Showgrounds (19th District
Agricultural Association) intends to enter into an agreement with Simulcast
Enterprises, Inc., to handle the transmission of simulcast wagering signals
to their showgrounds.  This contract is being negotiated by the California
Authority of Racing Fairs (on behalf of the 19th DAA) along with agreements
for several other fairs.

     At the same time, Allan Balch, president of Simulcast Enterprises, Inc., is
being offered a contract (as an individual) to manage a horse show for the
19th DAA in July.

     Mr. Balch is not an officer or employee of the 19th DAA and has no involvement
in the selection process for either of the contracts in question, nor will he
have any decision making authority as horse show manager that will impact in
anyway his own revenue as a result of his company's simulcasting work with
the 19th DAA.

     Your written opinion as to any potential conflict of interest on the part
of Mr. Balsh or the 19th DAA under the conditions stated, will be greatly
appreciated.  Please contact me if you require more information.


[signature]

Dave Terrill
Fair Management Consultant
Division of Fairs and Expositions
(916) 924-2108

jh


SURNAME

SO-106

California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

December 19, 1986

Dave Terrill
Fair Management Consultant
Division of Fairs and Expositions
1010 Hurley Way, Suite 200
Sacramento, CA 95825

Re: 86-337

Dear Mr. Terrill:

Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform
Act was received on December 15, 1986 by the Fair Political
Practices Commission. If you have any questions about your
advice request, you may contact Robert Leidigh, an attorney in
the Legal Division, directly at (916) 322-5901.

We try to answer all advice requests promptly. Therefore,
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions,
or more information is needed, you should expect a response
within 21 working days. You also should be aware that your
letter and our response are public records which may be
disclosed to the public upon receipt of a proper request for
disclosure.

Very truly yours,

Diane M. Griffiths
General Counsel
